specification.topic-statement: The send command delivers a text message to a named agent's mailbox and wakes the recipient via a best-effort PostgreSQL notification.

scope.in-scope: Argument parsing, message body size enforcement, in-memory recipient validation, database recipient status validation, mail insertion, and conditional notification.
scope.boundary: How the message is persisted in the database is handled by the mail storage subsystem. This topic invokes insertion but does not implement it.
The PostgreSQL NOTIFY mechanism is used to wake a waiting recipient. The notification channel semantics and consumption are out of scope.

field.name: recipient identifier
field.note: agent UUID or unique prefix; extracted from command arguments

behavior.argument-parsing:
The command arguments are parsed in the form: a recipient identifier followed by a double-quoted message body. Leading and trailing whitespace between the two parts is skipped.
The message body must be enclosed in double quotes. If the opening or closing quote is absent, a usage error is appended to the scrollback and the command completes without sending.
The message body is extracted verbatim from between the quotes. If the body length reaches 4095 bytes (not counting the closing quote), a size error is appended to the scrollback and the command completes without sending.
The maximum body length is enforced by a fixed-size stack buffer. A body of exactly 4095 characters is the maximum accepted; a body of 4096 or more bytes is rejected.
behavior.in-memory-recipient-lookup:
The recipient identifier is looked up in the current REPL's loaded agent list. If no matching agent is found, an error is appended to the scrollback and the command completes.
Agents from other sessions that are currently loaded in the REPL are eligible recipients. The in-memory lookup is not session-scoped.
behavior.database-recipient-validation: The recipient agent's record is retrieved from the database. If the agent does not exist in the database, an error is returned. If the agent's status is not "running", delivery is refused with a "recipient is dead" error. Only running agents may receive mail.
behavior.message-body-validation: An empty message body (zero bytes after parsing) is rejected. An error is returned without inserting any database record.
behavior.mail-insertion: A mail message is created with the sender's identifier, the recipient's identifier, the body text, and a timestamp set to the current Unix time. The message is inserted into the mail storage. If insertion fails, an error is returned.
behavior.recipient-notification:
After successful insertion, a PostgreSQL NOTIFY is sent on the channel named for the recipient agent, using the payload "mail". This wakes any recipient that is blocking in a wait operation.
The NOTIFY is sent only when the database connection is not currently inside a transaction. If the connection is in a transaction, the notification is silently skipped.
If the NOTIFY itself fails, the error is silently discarded. Message delivery via the mailbox is considered successful regardless of whether the notification was sent.
A recipient blocked in a wait operation discovers the message on its next polling interval (up to 50 milliseconds) if the notification was not delivered. No retry of the notification is attempted.
